subtopic_name: End-to-end hybrid Claude + local LLM research architecture
summary: A practical hybrid stock-research stack puts Claude Opus 4.7 (1M-token context, deep reasoning) at the planner/critic role and a local model such as Llama 3.1 8B (128k context, runs on Ollama) as the bulk worker for triage, summarization of filings, and chart pre-processing. Integration is via Model Context Protocol servers (spec 2025-11-25), an OpenAI-compatible LiteLLM gateway fronting 100+ providers, and shared file/SQLite artifacts. Iteration is driven by Claude Code hooks (PreToolUse, PostToolUse), the /loop skill, and headless SDK runs in GitHub Actions. Key pitfalls: numeric hallucination on prices and ratios, context-window blowups, tool-loop oscillation, and quote-mixing between models.

key_claims.claim: MCP spec version 2025-11-25 introduces async Tasks, server-side agent loops, and parallel tool calls, making it suitable as the integration spine between Claude Code and a local-LLM tool server.
An MCP server like mcp-local-llm lets Claude Code delegate mechanical tasks to a local model (Ollama or any OpenAI-compatible backend), with Claude reviewing and the local model producing volume.
LiteLLM provides a single OpenAI-compatible gateway to 100+ LLM providers, so a hybrid stack can swap between Claude, local Ollama, vLLM, or Bedrock without rewriting client code.
Claude Opus 4.7 ships with a 1M-token context window at standard API pricing and explicitly targets dense financial filings, charts, and long-running agentic workflows.
Llama 3.1 8B supports a 128k context window locally via Ollama but Ollama defaults to 2048 tokens unless explicitly configured, a frequent source of silent truncation in hybrid pipelines.

Claude Code hooks (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, plus prompt-based and agent-based hooks) run shell commands at lifecycle events, enabling deterministic iteration loops such as auto-formatting, validation gates, or kicking off local-LLM jobs after each Claude tool call.

Claude Code in headless mode (claude -p) runs the full agent loop from a single prompt and exits, which is the recommended pattern for scheduled SDK runs in GitHub Actions or cron-driven research jobs.

Without verified data infrastructure, mainstream LLMs hallucinate on up to 41% of finance-related queries, and one documented case had an LLM mis-stating Apple gross margin as 67% vs the actual 45%, causing a roughly $22B valuation error.
RAG-style grounding combined with tool calls reduces hallucinations by 60-80% by forcing the model to read prices and fundamentals from a trusted database rather than parametric memory.

The QuantMCP architecture pattern instructs the LLM not to answer factual financial queries from parametric memory and to fetch all data points via MCP-exposed tools (e.g. yfinance, Wind), which is the core anti-hallucination pattern for a TSLA-style research stack.

On consumer hardware (RTX 4070 Ti Super), local 22B-class models can match or beat Claude latency for short prompts (~1.4s vs ~2.1s) but cloud APIs win for long outputs because they sustain 60-80 tok/s vs 15-25 tok/s locally, which determines who should do bulk work vs deep reasoning.

recommended_charts.title: Context window: Claude Opus 4.7 vs Llama 3.1 8B vs Ollama default
Hallucination on ungrounded finance queries vs reduction with RAG/tools

limitations: Latency comparison numbers come from a single consumer-hardware benchmark and may not generalize; the 41% finance-hallucination figure is from an industry tutorial citing aggregate research rather than a single peer-reviewed paper, so the 'medium' confidence flag is applied. No direct head-to-head benchmark of a fully integrated Claude+local hybrid stock-research stack was found, so architecture patterns are synthesized from the component sources.
